# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Middleware } from '@vercel/node';
import * as cookie from 'cookie';

const middleware: Middleware = async (request, response) => {
  const cookies = request.headers.cookie;
  if (!cookies) {
    return response.status(401).send('Unauthorized');
  }

  const authToken = cookie.get('auth_token', cookies);
  if (authToken === null || authToken === undefined) {
    return response.status(401).send('Unauthorized');
  }

  // Check if the token is valid and authenticated
  const isAuthenticated = await validateToken(authToken);
  if (!isAuthenticated) {
    return response.status(401).send('Unauthorized');
  }

  // If authentication is successful, continue with the request
  return next();
};

async function validateToken(token: string): Promise<boolean> {
  // Check if the token is valid and authenticated
  // This will be an example implementation of how to check for a valid token
  const isValid = await someAuthenticationMethod(token);
  return isValid;
}
